# agentreflex

> agentreflex is the open commons of **reflexes** for AI coding agents. A reflex is
> deterministic logic that fires *before* an agent acts on a tool call — it can block,
> ask, modify, or pass. Write an instinct once and it compiles into every agent's
> native hook, so the same guardrail fires across Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot CLI,
> Gemini CLI, Windsurf, and OpenCode (and as an advisory AGENTS.md everywhere else).

## What it is

- **The category:** the missing third layer of agent tooling. Instructions (AGENTS.md)
  say what an agent should know; capabilities (MCP) say what it can do; **reflexes**
  (agentreflex) decide what actually happens the moment it acts.
- **A reflex is a folder, not a package.** Any folder with a `reflex.json` is a valid
  reflex; logic can be TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, or a shell script (the contract
  is JSON over stdin/stdout).
- **Decisions:** a reflex returns `pass`, `deny`, `ask`, or `modify`. Adapters
  translate that to each agent's native hook format.
- **Trust model:** reflexes run in your dev loop, so `agentreflex add` shows the code
  and the reflex's declared capabilities before wiring anything in, and the dispatcher
  fails open — it never blocks or crashes your agent because of its own error.

## Get started

- Install: `npx agentreflex init` wires every agent in your repo to your `.reflex/` folder.
- Add a reflex: `npx agentreflex add <name>` (catalog), `add github:user/repo`, `add ./path`, or `add https://…`.
- Packages: `agentreflex` (CLI), `@agentreflex/core` (runtime), `@agentreflex/adapters`. MIT licensed.

## Official reflexes (examples)

- no-force-push — blocks `git push --force` on shared branches
- no-rm-rf — blocks recursive `rm` of dangerous targets
- no-secrets — blocks reading or writing `.env` and credential files
- ask-on-prod — pauses before commands that deploy or publish to production
- stay-in-repo — blocks edits to files outside the project root
- conventional-commits — enforces Conventional Commits on commit messages
- recover — snapshots files before the agent edits them, so any change is undoable
- abide — declare your human↔agent working agreement once; deny/ask enforced everywhere

## Links

- Home: https://agentreflex.dev
- Why reflexes (essay): https://agentreflex.dev/blog/reflexes
- Docs: https://docs.agentreflex.dev
- Source & official reflexes: https://github.com/agentreflex/agentreflex
- Community catalog: https://github.com/agentreflex/awesome-reflexes
